varieties.name: Mazuma
varieties.origin: Wakayama Prefecture, Japan (former Mazuma Village); 1950s development; now widely cultivated in Shizuoka
varieties.traits: Purple-red stems and leaf bases, dark green heart-shaped leaves, longer slender rhizomes (6-12 inches), spreading growth habit, white edible flowers in spring
varieties.yield_notes: Takes 18-36 months to mature; slow-growing but highly prized for quality
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Nursery (QLD), Australian Plants Online, The Diggers Club. Usually available July-December. Limited commercial production in Tasmania only.

varieties.name: Daruma
varieties.origin: Shimane Prefecture, Japan; 1970s development; blue-stem type
varieties.traits: Green stems, dark green heart-shaped leaves, plumper shorter rhizomes than Mazuma, upright growth habit, grows faster (12-18 months)
varieties.yield_notes: 12-18 months to maturity; faster-growing than Mazuma; produces minimal offshoot branching; superior export quality

varieties.name: Green Thumb
varieties.origin: Taiwan development; cultivated successfully in Pacific Northwest USA
varieties.traits: Vibrant green color, larger leaves than other varieties, fast-growing (12-14 months), more disease-resistant
varieties.yield_notes: Faster maturity under certain conditions; particularly good variety for leaf production
varieties.availability: Not currently available from Australian suppliers; would need import as tissue culture or seed.

varieties.name: Shimane / Shimane No. 3
varieties.traits: Delicate spindle-shaped roots, spreading growth habit, tasty stems, leaves, and flowers
varieties.yield_notes: High quality rhizomes; susceptible to disease in humid conditions
varieties.availability: Not available in Australia; Japanese import only.
